title: Postman の 400 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are well-supported by primary sources like RFCs, MDN Web Docs, and Postman's official documentation.
The article provides accurate common causes and troubleshooting steps for 400 Bad Request errors.
Postman-specific tips are consistent with common debugging practices for the tool.
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty

sources.title: 400 Bad Request - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: The HTTP 400 Bad Request client error response status code indicates that the server would not process the request due to something the server considered to be a client error. The reason for a 400 response is typically due to malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing.

sources.title: 400 Bad Request Error: What It Means & How to Fix It - Elementor
sources.claim: A 400 Bad Request error is an HTTP status code that indicates the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. Common causes include URL string errors, corrupted browser cache and cookies, DNS cache issues, and uploading files that are too large.

sources.title: 400 Bad Request Error: What It Is and How to Fix It - Airbrake Docs
sources.claim: The 400 Bad Request Error is an HTTP response status code indicating that the server was unable to process the request sent by the client due to invalid syntax. If the client is sending a request to an invalid URL — particularly one that is malformed via improper characters — this could result in an http error 400.

sources.title: RFC 7231: Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP/1.1): Semantics and Content
sources.claim: The 400 (Bad Request) status code indicates that the server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax).

sources.title: 400 BAD request HTTP error code meaning? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: A 400 means that the request was malformed. In the case of a REST API with a JSON payload, 400's are typically, and correctly I would say, used to indicate that the JSON is invalid in some way according to the API specification for the service. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax.

sources.title: Fixing a 400 bad request error - Postman Docs
sources.claim: After sending a request, the response results in the error 400 Bad Request. A 400 Internal Server Error is an HTTP status code response that indicates that the server won't process the request because the syntax it received is perceived to be invalid or exceeds certain limitations set by the service provider. Make sure there is no error in the configuration of your request. Look for typos, whitespaces, or invalid JSON formatting. If your request contains special characters, try to turn off the encoding inside Postman.

sources.title: HTTP 400 error: What is it? - Link11
sources.claim: A HTTP 400 error: Bad Request is an HTTP status code that occurs when the server cannot understand the client's request due to errors on the client's side. URL Syntax Error: A common cause of the HTTP 400 status code is the presence of syntactic errors in the client's request. These can be typos or invalid characters.

sources.title: How to Understand and Resolve 400 Bad Request Errors - Interserver Tips
sources.claim: The 400 Bad Request error is an HTTP status code that serves as a notification that the server was unable to process the request due to a problem with the request itself. This error typically arises for a variety of reasons, including invalid request formatting, issues with the URL, incorrect HTTP headers, or unrecognized characters present in the request data. For example, using spaces instead of URL encoding can lead to a malformed request that the server cannot process, resulting in a 400 error. Invalid Query Parameters: When sending data in the URL (like for search queries), invalid or incorrectly formatted query parameters can trigger the 400 error. Incorrect HTTP Headers: Requests to the server often include various headers, such as Content-Type or User-Agent. If these headers are incorrectly defined or malformed, the server may not be able to interpret them correctly, resulting in a 400 error.

sources.title: Generate cURL commands using Postman Collection and OpenAPI spec - Medium
sources.claim: You can generate cURL commands from your Postman collections and OpenAPI specifications. In very simple terms, a curl command is a way to make HTTP requests from the command line.

sources.title: How to Convert a Postman Request to cURL? - GeeksforGeeks
sources.claim: Once you've set up your request, look for the 'Code' button located on the right side of the Postman interface, next to the request sending button. Click this button to open the code snippet window. In the code snippet window, you will see a dropdown menu. Click on it and select 'cURL' from the list of languages and tools.

sources.title: K000136172: How to get the cURL command equivalent for a Postman API call and viceversa? - MyF5 | Support
sources.claim: Open the request you want to convert to cURL command in Postman, then select the Code icon in the right sidebar.

sources.title: Encoding URLs in APIs: Avoid Broken Requests - The Product Guy
sources.claim: Improperly encoded URLs cause broken API calls. Learn when and how to encode URL segments vs query parameters, and how to debug 400 errors from bad encoding. A 400 Bad Request with no meaningful error message. The server receives a request, parses it, finds no matching data, and returns 200 OK with an empty result — or a 400 because it can't parse a malformed parameter.

sources.title: How to handle mismatched request body and content-type header?
sources.claim: If someone posts something with Content-Type 'application/json', but the post body has urlencoded syntax, the server should respond with HTTP 400: Bad Request.

sources.title: Why does Postman throw a 400 status for this POST request with a pre-request script?
sources.claim: Pre-request scripts in Postman run JavaScript before a request runs. If a POST request body is empty due to issues in a pre-request script, it can lead to a 400 error with a "Required request body is missing" message.

sources.title: cURL and Postman work wonderfully together
sources.claim: You can construct a request in Postman and convert it to cURL using the code snippet generator. You can import a cURL request into Postman and run it.

sources.title: Desktop Agent (MacOS) throwing 400 Bad Request but web agent seems ok - Help Hub
sources.claim: If the Postman Desktop Agent throws a 400 error while a cURL command for the same request works successfully in the terminal, it suggests a Postman-specific configuration issue (e.g., proxy, network).

sources.title: Status 400 error bad request in Postman SOLVED - YouTube
sources.claim: To solve status 400 error bad request in Postman, check the Content-Type header to ensure it is 'application/json' when sending JSON. Also, verify the JSON structure for correctness, such as ensuring no trailing commas and using double quotes for keys.

sources.title: 400 Bad Request - DecompressionFailureAtRequest | Apigee Edge
sources.claim: A 400 Bad Request can occur if the payload format sent by the client does not match the encoding specified in the Content-Encoding header.

sources.title: Response to web API requests: 400: Bad request - Adapty
sources.claim: A 400 Bad Request response means either the request is not valid JSON or some required field is missing. Correcting the JSON format and adding missing parameters is necessary to resolve it.

sources.title: [Solved] We could not parse the JSON body of your request. Ensure the API expects a JSON payload and verify its validity. Visit help.openai.com for assistance. - Portkey
sources.claim: A "We could not parse the JSON body of your request" error with a 400 status code indicates an invalid JSON payload, which could be due to incorrect formatting or missing quotes, brackets, or commas. It also suggests that the HTTP library might not be correctly configured to send the request with the Content-Type header set to application/json.

sources.title: Why Are Postman Environment Variables Not Resolving? - Prospera Soft
sources.claim: Users often encounter variable resolution issues in Postman due to incorrect syntax, undeclared variables, or overriding values. Ensuring all necessary variables are declared before reference can prevent these issues.

sources.title: postman giving 400 bad request when i use global varibales how to solve this
sources.claim: When using variables in Postman, they must be in proper JSON format, for example, strings should be enclosed in quotes like '"{{email}}"'. Incorrect formatting can lead to a 400 error.

sources.title: URL encoded colon resolves in 400 Bad Request - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Some servers, like ASP.net, may not allow certain characters (even if encoded, like a colon as %3A) in the URL before the query string, resulting in a 400 Bad Request error.

sources.title: Capture ResponseCode in the pre request - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: A pre-request script in Postman executes before the request is sent; therefore, it cannot access the response status code. However, errors in a pre-request script that lead to an invalid request can cause a 400 error.

sources.title: HTTP POST request end with bad request and JSON parse error - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: A server-side 'SyntaxError: Unexpected token in JSON' during parsing of a POST request body can result in a 400 Bad Request error.

sources.title: 400 Bad Request Errors: Understanding & Fixing Them - Ranked AI
sources.claim: A 400 Bad Request error indicates the server was unable to process the client's request due to incorrect syntax, invalid data, or a malformed request. Invalid or non-encoded characters in a URL can lead to a 400 error.

sources.title: Network Error Code 400: The Ultimate Guide to Fixing Bad Request Issues and Protecting Conversions - Webeyez Insights
sources.claim: Common causes of 400 Bad Request errors include malformed query strings, invalid characters or encoding, oversized payloads, missing or invalid parameters, and misconfigured headers.

sources.title: Updating environment variables not working - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: Inconsistent behavior when programmatically updating Postman environment variables, leading to old values being used and subsequent requests failing with 400 errors (e.g., 'invalid_grant').

unsupported_claims: Postmanで手動でURLを入力している場合、URLエンコードが自動的に行われないかもしれません。
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Clarify Postman's default URL encoding behavior for manually entered URLs, as the article implies it may not be automatic, while Postman documentation suggests it is, and sometimes needs to be turned off. Rephrase the claim if direct confirmation of non-automatic encoding for manual input cannot be found, focusing instead on the consequence of unencoded characters.
Optional improvement: Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
